meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Debugging for Student Developers
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills setup to conquer debugging. Elevate your student projects with this advanced guide using Antigravity and Claude CLI tools.
content.answer_block: Stop wrestling with cryptic stack traces. By integrating Claude Code into your terminal and leveraging Antigravity for architectural analysis, you transform debugging from a manual chore into an automated diagnostic workflow. This setup empowers you to pinpoint logic errors in seconds, accelerating your development velocity and deepening your technical expertise.
content.intro:
The screen is black, the cursor is blinking, and your console is bleeding red error messages. You have spent hours chasing a race condition that refuses to surface, and the frustration of being 'stuck' is the single biggest barrier between you and your first production-ready SaaS application. You are not failing; you are simply lacking the right diagnostic lens.

Today, we bridge the gap between student hobbyist and professional engineer. By mastering Claude Code, you gain an autonomous pair programmer that lives in your terminal, capable of parsing complex dependency chains and suggesting architectural fixes that would take a senior dev hours to isolate. Let’s calibrate your environment to turn debugging into a competitive advantage.

steps.title: Initialize Claude Code Environment
steps.description: Configure the Claude CLI to gain read/write access to your local project files, enabling the agent to understand your codebase context.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project structure and identify potential bottlenecks or anti-patterns that could lead to runtime exceptions.

steps.title: Contextual Error Injection Analysis
steps.description: Use Claude to trace the execution path of your failing function by piping the error logs directly into the terminal agent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the logic flow of the current module and highlight where state mutation might be occurring unexpectedly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix --file src/main.ts --error 'Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property'

steps.title: Automated Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Don't just fix the bug; build a safety net. Use Claude to generate tests that verify the fix holds under edge cases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for this module focusing on boundary conditions and input sanitization.

steps.title: Architectural Refactoring
steps.description: Use the agent to suggest structural improvements based on your specific framework, ensuring your code remains scalable as you add features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this component to follow SOLID principles and improve dependency injection patterns.

steps.title: Terminal-Integrated Documentation
steps.description: Ensure your fixes are documented in real-time so you can explain your decisions during technical interviews or portfolio reviews.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a markdown summary of the debugging session and the architectural changes implemented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write-docs --summary 'Fixing async race condition in user authentication'

pro_tips: Use '--fast' mode when debugging trivial syntax errors to reduce latency and token consumption.
Always provide the package.json file as context so Claude understands your library version constraints.
Create a .claudeignore file to prevent the agent from indexing large dependency folders or build artifacts.
Use the 'claude diff' command to review changes manually before applying them to your production branch.
Treat Claude as a junior dev: ask it to explain its reasoning before applying a fix to ensure you actually learn the underlying concept.
content.next_steps: Once your debugging workflow is automated, focus on deploying your project to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway to test your code in a production-like environment.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the diagnostic tools that separate amateur coders from professional software architects. Stop guessing, start diagnosing, and push your code to production with absolute confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
